---
id: "19.2.19"
title: "Nutanix AHV Host Capacity Headroom and Workload Placement"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.19 · Nutanix AHV Host Capacity Headroom and Workload Placement

## Description

Tracks Nutanix AHV hypervisor nodes for CPU and memory time-smoothed PPM, sums guest vCPU and memory from VM inventory for commit and oversubscription against logical processors, compares VM count to a simple density index, and applies a cluster-wide N-1 memory sufficiency test so operators see whether another node can absorb an evacuation in principle before a patch, distinct from CVM process health and distinct from a cluster-wide health score.

## Value

Capacity, placement, and change windows all need the same honest picture of per-host vCPU and memory, how far guest allocations are stretched across physical threads, and whether the cluster as a whole still has a memory cushion before you ask for a rolling host drain. A single, owner-routed row with freeze metadata keeps provisioning automation and app teams from overbooking a block during a week that already has a data-move, and it tells leadership when a hardware add or template shrink is a finance decision, not a late-night page.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunk_TA_nutanix, add the HEC poller and wrapper app for `nutanix_ahv_host_owner`, paste the `spl` into Search, then schedule every five minutes with `earliest=-1h@h` and route severities to your Nutanix and capacity queues. Add the optional acli side collector only if balloon or NUMA questions recur after the primary math is stable.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

Collect Splunk add-on for Nutanix (Splunk_TA_nutanix) on a modular-input or universal-forwarder tier with outbound TCP 9440 to Prism Element (or a Prism Central hop your TA documents), and on every Search Head that will run this use case, so `props.conf` and field discovery are consistent. Pin the TA in change control; a minor build can rename a JSON leaf from snake_case to camelCase, which is why the canonical SPL uses `coalesce()` at every user-visible hop.

Index: examples use `(index=hci OR index=nutanix)`; the value must be the same string your `inputs.conf` and `default/inputs.conf` from the TA set for 5076, and the role for operators must show that index in `srchIndexesAllowed`, not a legacy `main` only role.

Sourcetype families you must be able to see in Search before the alert is trusted: (1) `nutanix:host` from the vendor input that wraps Prism v2.0 `GET /hosts/` so each poll carries capacity and identity, including `num_cpu_cores`, `num_cpu_threads`, `memory_capacity_in_bytes`, `usage_stats` style hypervisor PPM, and a stable `host_uuid` (alias patterns vary). (2) `nutanix:vm` (and any TA alias) from `GET /vms/` (list or poll) for `num_vcpus`, memory fields, `power_state`, and the host or affinity pointer that places the guest on a node, usually `host_uuid` or a vendor synonym. (3) `nutanix:cluster` for cluster-wide totals, including optional `cluster_redundancy_state` and redundancy factor context when the MO path that backs your feed is a v2.0 or v1 cluster object your team maps into that sourcetype.

Custom HEC, five minute cadence, written by a small Python 3.11+ poller with TLS, secrets read from a 0600 file, and a single posting identity per cluster. The poller must call `GET https://<pe_fqdn>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/?include_vm_disk_config=false` (or the equivalent you already standardised) and `GET .../v2.0/vms/?include_vm_disk_config=false` for the VM inventory pass, and `GET .../rest/v1/cluster/` for a thin `cluster_redundancy_state` (failures-to-tolerate style) row you emit to `sourcetype=nutanix:cluster:redundancy` when the stock cluster stream in your build does not carry that field. Post JSON to HEC with `sourcetype` set to one of: `nutanix:host:capacity`, `nutanix:vm:placement`, `nutanix:cluster:redundancy` so the canonical SPL can choose the HEC line when a field is missing on the stock TA, without inventing a fake vendor sourcetype string.

The governance lookup `lookups/nutanix_ahv_host_owner.csv` and `transforms.conf` stanza `nutanix_ahv_host_owner` (filename matches the CSV) are mandatory for governance columns in the alert. Required columns, one row per node you care to page on: `host_uuid` (string, matches Prism host UUID for that node), `host_name` (optional human name for drilldown, may mirror Prism short name), `owner_team` (queue or mail-enabled group), `change_freeze_until` (ISO-8601 date or full timestamp, empty when no freeze is active), `target_max_vcpu_overcommit` (decimal, for example 4.0, meaning 4.0 vCPU allocated for every physical logical processor the hypervisor advertises, above which the row is treated as a policy breach in combination with other signals), `target_max_mem_commit` (decimal, for example 1.10, the planned maximum of sum-of-VM-allocated-guest-RAM to physical node RAM, above which the row is treated as commit pressure in combination with the hypervisor PPM and optional balloon signal), and `ftt_required` (small integer, how many node failures the service expects the cluster to tolerate; used only as a cross-check to `cluster_redundancy_state` in triage, not to replace the vendor object).

A wrapper app under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/`, for example `ta-nutanix-ahv-capacity-ctx`, should ship `default/transforms.conf` as:

[nutanix_ahv_host_owner]
filename = nutanix_ahv_host_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = true
min_matches = 0

and `metadata/local.meta` or `default.meta` that exports the transform to the role that runs the alert. Version-control the CSV; peer review every new row the same as a host firewall rule.

Optional, third stream: a Splunk add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunk_TA_nix) on a dedicated management host that can SSH to a place where your standards keep `ncli` or `acli`, scheduled through `inputs.conf` or `cron` to a wrapper script, emitting `sourcetype=nutanix:acli:host` for balloon and NUMA drill-down. This stream is not required to meet the first-run alert, but the detailed steps call it out in troubleshooting so capacity engineers do not conflate a missing optional stream with a broken primary feed.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

1A. Prove the vendor streams. Run `(index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:host | head 1` and read field names. Then run the same for `sourcetype=nutanix:vm` and `sourcetype=nutanix:cluster`. You must see `host_uuid` (or a synonym the SPL coalesces) on host and a stable join key to VM, or the join in Step 2 will be empty. If the TA is pointed at a Prism Central endpoint, confirm the cluster `name` you key in the join matches the HEC and CSV strings.

1B. HEC for the poller. On the indexer or HEC-receiving tier, add a `inputs.conf` stanza `[http://nutanix_capacity_hec]`, a token, the index, `indexes = hci` (or your split), and `sourcetype` defaults only if the poller omits the header; prefer explicit `sourcetype` in each HEC line from the poller. Set `useACK` in production, keep batch sizes under one megabyte, and add a 0600 `secrets` file for Basic auth to Prism, not inline flags.

1C. `props` for the custom sourcetypes. In the wrapper app, set `SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false` for the JSON you post, raise `TRUNCATE` to at least 1000000 for a large `usage_stats` blob, and add `KV_MODE = json` for each custom sourcetype. If a nested key lands as a literal with dots in the name, you can keep it and reference `usage_stats.hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm` in SPL, or promote it with a `FIELDALIAS` if your standards forbid dotted names.

1D. Optional `acli` side collector. Install Splunk_TA_nix on a Linux jump host that is allowed to call `acli` against one lab cluster, enable a scripted or modular input, and have the script `ssh` to a host where `acli` lives, run `acli host.list` and `acli host.get <uuid>`, and emit one JSON line per run with a `host_uuid` and optional balloon, swap, and locality counters your security team has approved. Index into the same `hci` index (or a capacity-only index) with `sourcetype=nutanix:acli:host`. This path is a deeper dive; do not block production rollout on the optional stream.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Copy the `spl` field in this use case into Search, set the time range to the same `earliest=-1h@h` window that you plan for the scheduled run, and confirm you see a row for each host in your pilot cluster. Save as a report named `rpt_uc_19_2_19_ahv_capacity_sanity` before you build an alert.

The first pipeline line after the time scope is a `comment` macro. If the macro is not present on a brownfield Search Head, add `macros.conf` in a private app, then restore the repository search text so audit logs still show a human reason string.

The join order matters: the outer arm stays at one row per `host_uuid` from `nutanix:host:capacity` (preferred) or the TA `nutanix:host` stream, then the `nutanix:vm` join fills `vcpu_alloc` and `mem_alloc_b`, then a cluster subsearch attaches `cluster_redundancy_state`, then `eventstats` computes the cluster sum of physical memory, sum of guest memory, and the largest single-host physical memory for the N-1 gate, `streamstats` orders hosts inside each cluster, and a final `inputlookup` join on `host_uuid` pulls owner and policy tunables. Severity uses `case()` with four string tiers, then a freeze-aware `eval` that only demotes `warning` and `info` when `change_freeze_until` is still in the future, which keeps capacity noise email-only during a freeze when the state is not already critical or high for utilization.

For saved search, use cron `*/5 * * * *`, time bounds `earliest = -1h@h` and `latest = @h` in `savedsearches.conf` (or the UI equivalent) so a slow poll in minute fifty-nine is still in window. Route `critical` to the on-call P1 path for the platform, `high` to ServiceNow P2, `warning` to the business-hours room, and `info` to a non-page dashboard only. Throttle on `host_uuid` for forty-five minutes, allow a new page when severity escalates a step.

Fenced for notebooks (text must match the `spl` field in this JSON file):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.19 — AHV host capacity, VM placement, N-1 memory safety. Tunable: target_max_vcpu_overcommit and target_max_mem_commit in nutanix_ahv_host_owner. Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until. Replace index= if your estate differs. earliest=-1h@h")`
(index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host:capacity" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host") earliest=-1h@h
| eval host_uuid=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, hostUuid, id, uuid, nodeUuid, node_uuid))
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name))
| eval hcpu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(usage_stats.hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, hypervisorCpuUsagePpm, "0")), 10)
| eval hmem=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(usage_stats.hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, hypervisorMemoryUsagePpm, "0")), 10)
| eval vcpu_pct_used=if((hcpu>0), round((hcpu/10000.0), 2), null())
| eval mem_pct_used=if((hmem>0), round((hmem/10000.0), 2), null())
| eval mem_cap_b=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(memory_capacity_in_bytes, memoryCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval nthr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_cpu_threads, numCpuThreads, "0")), 10)
| eval ncor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_cpu_cores, numCpuCores, "0")), 10)
| eval lproc=if((nthr>0), nthr, if((ncor>0), ncor*2, 0))
| eval n_vm_api=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_vms, numVms, "0")), 10)
| stats latest(_time) AS last_host_ev
        latest(cluster_name) AS cluster_name
        latest(vcpu_pct_used) AS vcpu_pct_used
        latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used
        latest(mem_cap_b) AS mem_cap_b
        latest(lproc) AS lproc
        latest(ncor) AS ncor
        latest(nthr) AS nthr
        latest(n_vm_api) AS n_vm_api
  BY host_uuid
| join type=left max=0 host_uuid
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:vm" OR sourcetype="nutanix:vm:placement") earliest=-1h@h
      | eval host_uuid=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, hostUuid, acropolisHostUuid, hostId))
      | eval pwr=lower(toString(coalesce(power_state, powerState, "on")))
      | where (like(pwr, "%on%")) OR (pwr="") OR (isnull(pwr))
      | eval nvc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(num_vcpus, numVCPUs, numVcpu, "0")), 10)
| eval bbytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_size_bytes, memorySizeBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval mb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_mb, memoryMb, "0")), 10)
| eval mmb2=if(bbytes>=2000000, bbytes, if(bbytes>0, bbytes*1048576, if(mb>0, mb*1048576, 0)))
      | where isnotnull(host_uuid) AND (len(host_uuid)>0)
      | stats sum(nvc) AS vcpu_alloc sum(mmb2) AS mem_alloc_b count AS vm_count BY host_uuid ]
| fillnull value=0 vcpu_alloc mem_alloc_b vm_count
| eval vcpu_overcommit_ratio=if((lproc>0) AND (isnotnull(vcpu_alloc)) AND (vcpu_alloc>=0), round((vcpu_alloc*1.0)/lproc, 3), null())
| eval mem_cap_mb=if((mem_cap_b>0), round((mem_cap_b/1048576.0), 1), null())
| eval mem_alloc_mb=if((mem_alloc_b>0), round((mem_alloc_b/1048576.0), 1), null())
| eval mem_commit_ratio=if(((isnotnull(mem_cap_mb)) AND (mem_alloc_mb>0) AND (mem_cap_mb>0)), round((mem_alloc_mb/mem_cap_mb), 3), null())
| eval vm_count=if((vm_count>0), vm_count, n_vm_api)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:redundancy" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster") earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(name, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster))
      | eval cluster_redundancy_state=toString(coalesce(cluster_redundancy_state, clusterRedundancyState, redundancyState, "unknown"))
      | stats latest(cluster_redundancy_state) AS cluster_redundancy_state BY cluster_name ]
| eventstats sum(mem_cap_b) AS cluster_phys_mem_b sum(mem_alloc_b) AS cluster_alloc_mem_b max(mem_cap_b) AS max_host_mem_b BY cluster_name
| eval cluster_spare_b=if((isnotnull(cluster_phys_mem_b)) AND (isnotnull(cluster_alloc_mem_b)), (cluster_phys_mem_b - cluster_alloc_mem_b), null())
| eval n1_mem_ok=if((isnotnull(cluster_spare_b)) AND (isnotnull(max_host_mem_b)) AND (max_host_mem_b>0) AND (cluster_spare_b>=max_host_mem_b), 1, 0)
| eval headroom_for_n_minus_1=n1_mem_ok
| streamstats count AS cl_host_seq BY cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 host_uuid
    [ inputlookup nutanix_ahv_host_owner
    | eval host_uuid=toString(host_uuid) 
    | eval change_freeze_until=toString(change_freeze_until)
    | fields + host_uuid, host_name, owner_team, change_freeze_until, target_max_vcpu_overcommit, target_max_mem_commit, ftt_required ]
| eval tgt_vc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vcpu_overcommit, "4.0")), 10)
| eval tgt_m=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_mem_commit, "1.10")), 10)
| eval freeze_ts=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(freeze_ts)) AND (now()<freeze_ts), 1, 0)
| eval vcap_h=lproc*2.0
| eval vm_count_vs_max=if((vcap_h>0) AND (vm_count>=0), round((vm_count/vcap_h), 3), null())
| eval mem_policy_hit=if(((isnotnull(mem_commit_ratio)) AND (isnotnull(tgt_m)) AND (mem_commit_ratio>tgt_m)), 1, 0)
| eval vcpu_policy_hit=if(((isnotnull(vcpu_overcommit_ratio)) AND (isnotnull(tgt_vc)) AND (vcpu_overcommit_ratio>tgt_vc)), 1, 0)
| eval policy_breach=if(((mem_policy_hit=1) OR (vcpu_policy_hit=1)), 1, 0)
| eval alert_sev=case(
    (n1_mem_ok=0) OR (policy_breach=1), "critical",
    (vcpu_pct_used>92) OR (mem_pct_used>92), "high",
    (vcpu_pct_used>78) OR (mem_pct_used>82) OR ((isnotnull(mem_commit_ratio)) AND (mem_commit_ratio>1.0)), "warning",
    1=1, "info"
  )
| eval alert_sev=if((freeze_active=1) AND (alert_sev="warning"), "info", if((freeze_active=1) AND (alert_sev="info"), "info", alert_sev))
| eval sevord=case(alert_sev="critical", 4, alert_sev="high", 3, alert_sev="warning", 2, 1, 1)
| sort 0 - sevord, cluster_name, host_uuid
| table host_uuid, cluster_name, host_name, owner_team, vcpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, vcpu_overcommit_ratio, mem_commit_ratio, vm_count, vm_count_vs_max, headroom_for_n_minus_1, cluster_redundancy_state, mem_cap_mb, mem_alloc_mb, cluster_spare_b, max_host_mem_b, mem_policy_hit, vcpu_policy_hit, alert_sev, change_freeze_until, freeze_active, last_host_ev, cl_host_seq, policy_breach
```

Step 3 — Validate

Open Prism Element for the same cluster. Under Hardware, Hosts, pick one node and read hypervisor memory and CPU PPM, thread count, and RAM, then match `vcpu_pct_used`, `mem_pct_used`, and `lproc` in Splunk. Under VM, open the table view, filter on that node, and add guest vCPUs and memory; your Splunk-joined `vcpu_alloc` and `mem_alloc_mb` should be within a small margin of the UI, allowing for powered-off guests. Use Analysis only as a second opinion for burst CPU; this use case is about headroom, not a single five-minute spike.

Compare the cluster object in Splunk to Hardware, Cluster, for the redundancy or fault-tolerance readout, not to the 0-100 health score in UC-19.2.25, which is a different signal. The host-capacity result should not be mistaken for the controller-service or CVM process story in UC-19.2.7 or UC-19.2.14; when both fire, triage the CVM path if guests are at risk, and keep this UC for placement.

Sanity band: a lab node that is not in DRS move state should not show `headroom_for_n_minus_1=0` for weeks at a time unless the cluster is genuinely tight; if that happens, cross-check the poller is not double-counting memory from a duplicate HEC run. NTP skew between the poller and Splunk makes `_time` look odd but not the `latest()` math if the poll is healthy.

Faking data: a controlled HEC line that posts a synthetic `host_uuid` in the `nutanix:host:capacity` sourcetype with `memory_capacity_in_bytes` and `num_cpu_threads` set to known values, then a few `nutanix:vm:placement` lines that reference the same `host_uuid`, lets you test severity without touching production hardware. Remove synthetic rows before monthly evidence exports.

Field hygiene notes: when `usage_stats` arrives empty but top-level PPM fields exist, the first `coalesce` branch still works; if both are empty, `vcpu_pct_used` is null and the row is still useful for the commit and N-1 math from VM and capacity bytes, which is a deliberate choice so you can detect a poll with missing PPM in a child panel with `isnull(vcpu_pct_used)` without hiding placement risk. When Prism returns `memory_size_bytes` in MiB in one API and bytes in another, the VM join uses a threshold under two million to multiply by 1048576, which is a coarse guard that is safe for normal VM sizes; if your lab has tiny 256 MiB test guests, raise the split threshold after you document the API unit in your poller. When a cluster is mid-storage-only rebalance, memory commit does not change for guests, but the business may still ask for a pause in new templates; the freeze column covers that case without a second alert.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboards: a top row of single values for hosts with `alert_sev=critical` and `headroom_for_n_minus_1=0`, a sortable `table` of the last hop with owner columns, a cluster-level column chart of `sum(mem_alloc_b)` to `sum(mem_cap_b)` (child search) for finance-facing capacity, and a scatter of `vcpu_overcommit_ratio` by `mem_commit_ratio` to spot skewed nodes. Add a frozen indicator column so CAB can see when a team asked for quiet email without hiding true critical placement risk.

Runbook: step one, confirm the host is not in maintenance with a `nutanix:host` field read in a drilldown, because maintenance suppresses new placement but the math here still matters for rollback. Step two, if `alert_sev=critical` for memory commit or N-1, open vRealize Automation, Terraform, or Calm to freeze new large-template deploys to that cluster until a rebalance is scheduled, and add a Jira to the same queue that owns the Nutanix cluster object in CMDB. Step three, pair the ticket with the CVM liveness checks in UC-19.2.7 and the CVM service pressure in UC-19.2.14, because a narrow storage path can block evacuation even when CPU math says there is headroom. Step four, pair with UC-19.2.10 for version and LCM posture before you promise a new hardware node in the same change window, because a blocked one-click is not the same as empty U-space on disk.

Cadence: weekly thirty-minute review of this dashboard with capacity planning, and a deep dive when `cluster_spare_b` trend crosses a finance-approved floor. ITSI optional: one generic service entity per `cluster_name` and KPIs on `n1_mem_ok` and `mem_commit_ratio` with static thresholds you tune after two sprints. Automation hooks: vRealize Automation, Terraform, and Calm each expose APIs that a provisioning gate can call to read a CSV export from the same `table` this search produces, or a summary index you build with `outputlookup` every five minutes; the important contract is the same `host_uuid` key your IPAM and server CMDB use, not the display name.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(1) Empty result rows: if the `nutanix:host` or `nutanix:host:capacity` subsearch is empty, the modular input is not running, the index is wrong, or 9440 is blocked. Read `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunk_ta_nutanix*.log` and fix network, not the SPL first.

(2) Stale data: if `last_host_ev` is more than two poll intervals behind clock time, check HEC 400 lines in `_internal`, or the poller log for HTTP 401/503, or a Prism Element restart that your script does not back off for.

(3) Balloon false-positive: when the optional `nutanix:acli:host` stream shows a large balloon and guest swap while `mem_pct_used` in Splunk is still low, the guest is over-committed, not the hypervisor only; do not use `mem_commit_ratio` alone to close the ticket, validate with the guest team or a guest-metric add-on, and add a runbook line that acli is advisory.

(4) NUMA mis-attribution: a narrow CPU figure on a single socket while the other is idle can look like an overcommit in aggregate when it is a locality problem; the optional acli and guest NUMA counter stream is the next hop, and live migration is out of band for this use case, but the false sense of `vcpu_overcommit_ratio` is real until you add per-NUMA-node metrics.

(5) vCPU overcommit ratio interpretation: a ratio of 6 on a batch cluster may be expected, while 2.5 on a latency-sensitive VDI cluster may be too high. Always compare to `target_max_vcpu_overcommit` in the lookup, not a global constant, and document why a higher ratio is safe when the workload is idle most of the day.

(6) `cluster_redundancy_state` mis-mapping: if your poller to `nutanix:cluster:redundancy` is pointed at a different cluster UUID than the host stream, the column shows `unknown` or a stale value; align API paths and the `name` string used in the join, or add a `cluster_uuid` `eval` in both sides.

(7) Oversized VM blocks maintenance: when a single guest is larger than any remaining node free space, N-1 math says `n1_mem_ok=0` even if the total cluster spare is high; the fix is to shrink the template, add a node, or use storage live migration, not to silence the search.

(8) HEC double ingest: if two pollers run the same five-minute job, memory sums double; fix is a single poller and an ACK token, not a `dedup` in production.

(9) Lookup typos: if `host_uuid` in the CSV is missing a character from Prism UUID, the `join` to `inputlookup` leaves owner columns null; the alert still works but you lose pager routing.

(10) AHV only vs mixed hypervisor: if one legacy ESXi node remains in a cluster, thread counts and memory accounting differ; note that in the CSV and consider a per-row flag if you add a `hypervisor` column in a later revision. Cross-team reviews should keep the UC-19.2.7 and UC-19.2.25 dashboards in separate tabs; merged SPL in one search makes ownership and paging noisy.

Additional capacity narrative for large estates: when you exceed roughly fifty hosts per cluster, consider a pre-summary `outputlookup` keyed on `host_uuid` every five minutes to shrink interactive search time, and keep the same `comment` line in the summary definition. When a storage-only incident from another UC is open, annotate the capacity review with a link; guests may not move even when CPU shows room. When finance asks for a three-year TCO, export this table monthly with cluster name, not only host, and join business service from a second lookup if you outgrow a single `owner_team` column.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.19 — AHV host capacity, VM placement, N-1 memory safety. Tunable: target_max_vcpu_overcommit and target_max_mem_commit in nutanix_ahv_host_owner. Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until. Replace index= if your estate differs. earliest=-1h@h")`
(index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host:capacity" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host") earliest=-1h@h
| eval host_uuid=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, hostUuid, id, uuid, nodeUuid, node_uuid))
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name))
| eval hcpu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(usage_stats.hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, hypervisorCpuUsagePpm, "0")), 10)
| eval hmem=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(usage_stats.hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, hypervisorMemoryUsagePpm, "0")), 10)
| eval vcpu_pct_used=if((hcpu>0), round((hcpu/10000.0), 2), null())
| eval mem_pct_used=if((hmem>0), round((hmem/10000.0), 2), null())
| eval mem_cap_b=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(memory_capacity_in_bytes, memoryCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval nthr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_cpu_threads, numCpuThreads, "0")), 10)
| eval ncor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_cpu_cores, numCpuCores, "0")), 10)
| eval lproc=if((nthr>0), nthr, if((ncor>0), ncor*2, 0))
| eval n_vm_api=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_vms, numVms, "0")), 10)
| stats latest(_time) AS last_host_ev
        latest(cluster_name) AS cluster_name
        latest(vcpu_pct_used) AS vcpu_pct_used
        latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used
        latest(mem_cap_b) AS mem_cap_b
        latest(lproc) AS lproc
        latest(ncor) AS ncor
        latest(nthr) AS nthr
        latest(n_vm_api) AS n_vm_api
  BY host_uuid
| join type=left max=0 host_uuid
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:vm" OR sourcetype="nutanix:vm:placement") earliest=-1h@h
      | eval host_uuid=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, hostUuid, acropolisHostUuid, hostId))
      | eval pwr=lower(toString(coalesce(power_state, powerState, "on")))
      | where (like(pwr, "%on%")) OR (pwr="") OR (isnull(pwr))
      | eval nvc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(num_vcpus, numVCPUs, numVcpu, "0")), 10)
| eval bbytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_size_bytes, memorySizeBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval mb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_mb, memoryMb, "0")), 10)
| eval mmb2=if(bbytes>=2000000, bbytes, if(bbytes>0, bbytes*1048576, if(mb>0, mb*1048576, 0)))
      | where isnotnull(host_uuid) AND (len(host_uuid)>0)
      | stats sum(nvc) AS vcpu_alloc sum(mmb2) AS mem_alloc_b count AS vm_count BY host_uuid ]
| fillnull value=0 vcpu_alloc mem_alloc_b vm_count
| eval vcpu_overcommit_ratio=if((lproc>0) AND (isnotnull(vcpu_alloc)) AND (vcpu_alloc>=0), round((vcpu_alloc*1.0)/lproc, 3), null())
| eval mem_cap_mb=if((mem_cap_b>0), round((mem_cap_b/1048576.0), 1), null())
| eval mem_alloc_mb=if((mem_alloc_b>0), round((mem_alloc_b/1048576.0), 1), null())
| eval mem_commit_ratio=if(((isnotnull(mem_cap_mb)) AND (mem_alloc_mb>0) AND (mem_cap_mb>0)), round((mem_alloc_mb/mem_cap_mb), 3), null())
| eval vm_count=if((vm_count>0), vm_count, n_vm_api)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:redundancy" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster") earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(name, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster))
      | eval cluster_redundancy_state=toString(coalesce(cluster_redundancy_state, clusterRedundancyState, redundancyState, "unknown"))
      | stats latest(cluster_redundancy_state) AS cluster_redundancy_state BY cluster_name ]
| eventstats sum(mem_cap_b) AS cluster_phys_mem_b sum(mem_alloc_b) AS cluster_alloc_mem_b max(mem_cap_b) AS max_host_mem_b BY cluster_name
| eval cluster_spare_b=if((isnotnull(cluster_phys_mem_b)) AND (isnotnull(cluster_alloc_mem_b)), (cluster_phys_mem_b - cluster_alloc_mem_b), null())
| eval n1_mem_ok=if((isnotnull(cluster_spare_b)) AND (isnotnull(max_host_mem_b)) AND (max_host_mem_b>0) AND (cluster_spare_b>=max_host_mem_b), 1, 0)
| eval headroom_for_n_minus_1=n1_mem_ok
| streamstats count AS cl_host_seq BY cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 host_uuid
    [ inputlookup nutanix_ahv_host_owner
    | eval host_uuid=toString(host_uuid) 
    | eval change_freeze_until=toString(change_freeze_until)
    | fields + host_uuid, host_name, owner_team, change_freeze_until, target_max_vcpu_overcommit, target_max_mem_commit, ftt_required ]
| eval tgt_vc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vcpu_overcommit, "4.0")), 10)
| eval tgt_m=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_mem_commit, "1.10")), 10)
| eval freeze_ts=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(freeze_ts)) AND (now()<freeze_ts), 1, 0)
| eval vcap_h=lproc*2.0
| eval vm_count_vs_max=if((vcap_h>0) AND (vm_count>=0), round((vm_count/vcap_h), 3), null())
| eval mem_policy_hit=if(((isnotnull(mem_commit_ratio)) AND (isnotnull(tgt_m)) AND (mem_commit_ratio>tgt_m)), 1, 0)
| eval vcpu_policy_hit=if(((isnotnull(vcpu_overcommit_ratio)) AND (isnotnull(tgt_vc)) AND (vcpu_overcommit_ratio>tgt_vc)), 1, 0)
| eval policy_breach=if(((mem_policy_hit=1) OR (vcpu_policy_hit=1)), 1, 0)
| eval alert_sev=case(
    (n1_mem_ok=0) OR (policy_breach=1), "critical",
    (vcpu_pct_used>92) OR (mem_pct_used>92), "high",
    (vcpu_pct_used>78) OR (mem_pct_used>82) OR ((isnotnull(mem_commit_ratio)) AND (mem_commit_ratio>1.0)), "warning",
    1=1, "info"
  )
| eval alert_sev=if((freeze_active=1) AND (alert_sev="warning"), "info", if((freeze_active=1) AND (alert_sev="info"), "info", alert_sev))
| eval sevord=case(alert_sev="critical", 4, alert_sev="high", 3, alert_sev="warning", 2, 1, 1)
| sort 0 - sevord, cluster_name, host_uuid
| table host_uuid, cluster_name, host_name, owner_team, vcpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, vcpu_overcommit_ratio, mem_commit_ratio, vm_count, vm_count_vs_max, headroom_for_n_minus_1, cluster_redundancy_state, mem_cap_mb, mem_alloc_mb, cluster_spare_b, max_host_mem_b, mem_policy_hit, vcpu_policy_hit, alert_sev, change_freeze_until, freeze_active, last_host_ev, cl_host_seq, policy_breach
```

## Visualization

Table of host_uuid, cluster_name, vcpu and memory headroom, commit ratios, N-1 flag, and severity, color by alert_sev. Single value: count of hosts with alert_sev=critical. Bar chart: mem_commit_ratio by host. Scatter: vcpu_overcommit_ratio versus mem_commit_ratio. Timechart: cluster_spare_b from a child summary. Optional panel: inputlookup preview for the governance CSV to prove freshness in audits.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (Splunkbase 5076)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)
- [Nutanix Developer — Prism Gateway REST reference](https://www.nutanix.dev/reference/prism-gw-rapis/)
